summary: Gruber reviews leather case options for the iPhone 15 Pro, highlighting Stephen Robles's video comparison of 11 cases and sharing his own preferences. He's been a loyal Bullstrap customer for years but acknowledges the nearly identical Ryan London case may be the better value at $30 less. He details his specific requirements: high-quality leather, no bottom lip, good buttons, and a gentle camera hump. He also offers a surprising partial defense of Apple's maligned FineWoven cases, noting the material is fine but the bottom lip design is a dealbreaker.
key_points: Bullstrap and Ryan London cases are nearly identical — same shape, buttons, and camera hump — likely made by the same Chinese manufacturer, but Ryan London is $30 cheaper
Gruber strongly prefers cases without a bottom lip for better swipe-up feel and USB-C cable compatibility, a design that's becoming increasingly rare
Apple's FineWoven cases aren't as bad as their reputation suggests — the material is acceptable, but the bottom lip design ruins them for Gruber
A chance encounter with The Verge's Dan Seifert confirmed that the Ryan London leather has a slightly better feel than Bullstrap
money_quotes: I strongly prefer the way a no-bottom-lip case feels when swiping up from the bottom of the phone — an action I perform dozens of times a day.
I don't know what the deal is between these two companies, but their cases are nearly identical.
I suspect both companies use the same manufacturer in China.
An encased iPhone feels more like a camera to me, with better grippiness.
The dealbreaker for me is not the FineWoven material, which I actually kind of like. Rather, it's the bottom lip.

You can't go wrong with either of them — they really are remarkably similar cases overall, almost certainly made by the same Chinese manufacturer — but given that it's $30 cheaper, I suppose I'd recommend the Ryan London.
key_insight: The best iPhone cases prioritize tactile daily ergonomics — like a cutout bottom lip for swiping — over maximum protection, and two nearly identical leather cases from different brands suggest the real product differentiation is in branding and price, not manufacturing.
